580	//
581	//   - project provided (ID or path): resolve via resolveProject; 400-equivalent
582	//     error result if not found.
583	//   - project omitted + exactly one project registered: auto-resolve (singleton
584	//     compat — the common single-project deployment).
585	//   - project omitted + >1 project registered: return a disambiguation error.
586	//   - project omitted + 0 projects registered: fall back to s.WorkingDir.
587	//
588	// The error mcpToolResult (if non-nil) should be returned directly to the MCP
589	// client; workingDir is empty when an error is returned.
590	func (s *Server) mcpResolveWorkingDir(project string) (string, *mcpToolResult) {
591		if project != "" {
592			entry, ok := s.resolveProject(project)
593			if !ok {
594				return "", &mcpToolResult{
595					Content: []mcpContent{mcpText(fmt.Sprintf("project not found: %s", project))},
596					IsError: true,
597				}
598			}
599			return entry.Path, nil
600		}
601		projects := s.state.GetProjects()
602		switch len(projects) {
603		case 0:
604			return s.WorkingDir, nil
605		case 1:
606			return projects[0].Path, nil
607		default:
608			return "", &mcpToolResult{
609				Content: []mcpContent{mcpText("multiple projects registered; specify 'project' argument (id or path)")},
610				IsError: true,
611			}
612		}
613	}
614	
615	// projectScoped is middleware for /api/projects/{project}/... routes.
616	// It pulls {project} out of the path, resolves it to a registered ProjectEntry,
617	// and injects the entry into the request context. Requests whose project does
618	// not resolve get 404.
619	func (s *Server) projectScoped(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
620		return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
621			key := r.PathValue("project")
622			entry, ok := s.resolveProject(key)
623			if !ok {
624				writeJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{"error": "project not found"})
625				return
626			}
627			next(w, r.WithContext(withProjectContext(r.Context(), entry)))
628		}
629	}
630	
631	// singletonScoped is middleware for legacy /api/... routes. When exactly one
632	// project is registered, it injects that project into the request context so
633	// legacy handlers behave identically to the scoped /api/projects/{id}/...
634	// equivalent. When zero or more than one project is registered the request
635	// passes through unchanged, preserving the historical multi-project aggregate
636	// behavior of list endpoints and the server's own WorkingDir default for
637	// single-item endpoints.
638	func (s *Server) singletonScoped(next http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {
639		return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
640			projects := s.state.GetProjects()
641			if len(projects) == 1 {
642				r = r.WithContext(withProjectContext(r.Context(), projects[0]))
643			}
644			next(w, r)
645		}
646	}
647	
648	// workingDirForRequest returns the filesystem root this request should operate
649	// against. Scoped routes get the resolved project's path; unscoped routes fall
650	// back to the server's own WorkingDir.
651	func (s *Server) workingDirForRequest(r *http.Request) string {
652		if p, ok := projectFromContext(r.Context()); ok {
653			return p.Path
654		}
655		return s.WorkingDir
656	}
657	
658	// libraryPathForRequest returns the library path for the request's project.
659	func (s *Server) libraryPathForRequest(r *http.Request) string {
660		return s.libraryPathFor(s.workingDirForRequest(r))
661	}
662	
663	// beadStoreForRequest returns a bead store rooted at the request's project.
664	func (s *Server) beadStoreForRequest(r *http.Request) *bead.Store {
665		return bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(s.workingDirForRequest(r), ".ddx"))
666	}
667	
668	// buildDocGraphForRequest builds the docgraph from the request's project root.
669	func (s *Server) buildDocGraphForRequest(r *http.Request) (*docgraph.Graph, error) {
670		return docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig(s.workingDirForRequest(r))
671	}
672	
673	// execStoreForRequest returns an exec store rooted at the request's project.
674	func (s *Server) execStoreForRequest(r *http.Request) *ddxexec.Store {
675		return ddxexec.NewStore(s.workingDirForRequest(r))
676	}
677	
678	// workerManagerForRequest returns the live WorkerManager when the request
679	// resolves to the server's own WorkingDir, and a read-only manager rooted at
680	// the request's project otherwise. Requests with no project context fall back
681	// to s.workers.
682	func (s *Server) workerManagerForRequest(r *http.Request) *WorkerManager {
683		dir := s.workingDirForRequest(r)
684		if dir == s.WorkingDir {
685			return s.workers
686		}
687		return NewWorkerManager(dir)
688	}

690	// route registers a handler and records the pattern for test introspection.
691	func (s *Server) route(pattern string, handler http.HandlerFunc) {
692		s.routePatterns = append(s.routePatterns, pattern)
693		s.mux.HandleFunc(pattern, handler)
694	}
695	
696	func (s *Server) routes() {
697		// Health — no trust gate.
698		s.route("GET /api/health", s.handleHealth)
699		s.route("GET /api/ready", s.handleReady)
700	
701		// Every other endpoint is gated on isTrusted() via the requireTrusted
702		// middleware. If you add a new route, use trusted() so the gate test
703		// (TestAllNonHealthHandlersGateOnIsTrusted) stays green.
704		trusted := func(pattern string, handler http.HandlerFunc) {
705			s.route(pattern, requireTrusted(handler))
706		}
707		// Legacy unscoped routes auto-resolve to the single registered project
708		// (singleton compatibility). When more than one project is registered the
709		// request passes through unchanged — list endpoints keep their
710		// cross-project aggregation behavior, single-item endpoints keep their
711		// s.WorkingDir default.
712		legacy := func(pattern string, handler http.HandlerFunc) {
713			trusted(pattern, s.singletonScoped(handler))
714		}
715		// Project-scoped routes extract {project} from the URL, resolve it, and
716		// inject the ProjectEntry into the request context.
717		scoped := func(pattern string, handler http.HandlerFunc) {
718			trusted(pattern, s.projectScoped(handler))
719		}
720

4671	func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) {
4672		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
4673		w.WriteHeader(status)
4674		enc := json.NewEncoder(w)
4675		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
4676		_ = enc.Encode(v)
4677	}

4688	// --- GraphQL Endpoints ---
4689	
4690	func (s *Server) handleGraphQLQuery(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
4691		// Gate /graphql on the same localhost auth that protects every /api/*
4692		// handler. Without this, operators running `ddx server --addr 0.0.0.0`
4693		// ship an unauthenticated GraphQL endpoint serving bead data, worker
4694		// logs, mutations, and subscriptions. See opus final-gate review of
4695		// ddx-02d6142d and the scope line "isTrusted() is still the gatekeeper;
4696		// no auth bypass introduced".
4697		if !isTrusted(r) {
4698			writeJSON(w, http.StatusForbidden, map[string]string{"error": "graphql is localhost-only"})
4699			return
4700		}
4701		s.serveGraphQL(w, r, s.WorkingDir)
4702	}
4703	
4704	// handleGraphQLQueryScoped serves /api/projects/{project}/graphql. The
4705	// projectScoped middleware has already resolved {project} into the request
4706	// context (or written 404 if unknown). LAYER 1 of the GraphQL multi-project
4707	// fix: per-request resolver reconstruction with the resolved project's
4708	// WorkingDir, so DocumentByPath and friends do not leak across projects.
4709	// Closes ddx-4c51d33e.
4710	func (s *Server) handleGraphQLQueryScoped(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
4711		dir := s.workingDirForRequest(r)
4712		s.serveGraphQL(w, r, dir)
4713	}
4714	
4715	// serveGraphQL builds a fresh gqlgen handler whose Resolver is rooted at
4716	// workingDir and serves the request through it. Constructed per request so
4717	// LAYER 1's scoped route can isolate each project's WorkingDir without the
4718	// deeper context-threading refactor (deferred to LAYER 2 / ddx-055e8d32).
4719	func (s *Server) serveGraphQL(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, workingDir string) {
4720		var fedProvider ddxgraphql.FederationProvider
4721		if s.hub != nil {
4722			fedProvider = newHubFederationProvider(s)
4723		}
4724		gqlServer := handler.New(ddxgraphql.NewExecutableSchema(ddxgraphql.Config{
4725			Resolvers: &ddxgraphql.Resolver{
4726				State:                              s.state,
4727				WorkingDir:                         workingDir,
4728				Workers:                            s.workers,
4729				BeadBus:                            s.beadHub,
4730				Actions:                            &workerDispatchAdapter{manager: s.workers},
4731				ExecLogs:                           &execLogAdapter{workingDir: workingDir},
4732				CoordMetrics:                       &coordMetricsAdapter{reg: s.workers.LandCoordinators},
4733				CSRFTokens:                         s.csrfTokens,
4734				OperatorPromptIdempotency:          s.operatorPromptIdempotency,
4735				OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist: s.operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist,
4736				PromptCapBytes:                     serverPromptCapBytes,
4737				BuildSHA:                           serverBuildSHA(),
4738				NodeID:                             s.state.Node.ID,
4739				Federation:                         fedProvider,
4740			},
4741			Directives: ddxgraphql.DirectiveRoot{},
4742		}))
4743		// Plumb the originating *http.Request into the resolver context so
4744		// resolvers (e.g. operatorPromptSubmit) can validate the CSRF header
4745		// and capture the originating identity from headers tsnetMiddleware
4746		// injects (X-Tailscale-User / X-Tailscale-Node).
4747		r = r.WithContext(ddxgraphql.WithHTTPRequest(r.Context(), r))
4748		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
4749		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
4750		gqlServer.AddTransport(transport.Websocket{
4751			Upgrader: websocket.Upgrader{
4752				CheckOrigin: func(r *http.Request) bool { return true },
4753			},
4754			KeepAlivePingInterval: 30 * time.Second,
4755		})
4756	
4757		gqlServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)
4758	}

{"acceptance":"1. ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md exists, named appropriately, committed.
2. ADR explicitly evaluates Proposals A, B, C (or articulates a fourth) and documents the chosen design with rationale.
3. ADR includes the worker-server API contract: full request/response schemas for register, heartbeat, next-bead, event, result, disconnect.
4. ADR includes sequence diagrams (text/mermaid) for: worker registration, bead claim, execute+report, server-restart recovery, graceful shutdown.
5. ADR includes a compatibility analysis: what existing tests break, what behaviors migrate, what's a breaking change for operators.
6. ADR specifies how --local mode collapses into the same code path (in-process API impl).
7. ADR specifies sequencing relative to: ddx-29058e2a (spec unification), ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor children, ddx-dc157075 (stay-alive — likely subsumed).
8. ADR includes implementation roadmap as an ordered list (with proposed bead titles, NOT actual filed beads — those come after ADR review).

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

INTEGRATION WITH IN-FLIGHT WORK

This TD intersects:
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): the spec becomes the registration payload — single struct still applies
- ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor epic: the work package boundary aligns with worker autonomy; C9 (StopCondition) becomes worker-side state
- ddx-4c51d33e (cross-project leak): workers identify their project at registration; server enforces project boundaries via session token
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): becomes natural — worker stays alive, polls for work, doesn't exit on empty queue

IMPLEMENTATION OUTLINE (post-TD)
1. Define API contract + handlers (cli/internal/server/worker_api.go)
2. Add worker client (cli/internal/agent/work/client.go) implementing the API
3. Add in-process API implementation for --local mode (cli/internal/agent/work/local_api.go)
4. Migrate execute_bead_loop / drain_loop to consume the API instead of direct store access
5. Migrate server worker spawn to register-then-poll instead of inline-execution

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS TD
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or new FEAT) describing the worker contract
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups

DEPS
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads","id":"ddx-076147ee","issue_type":"epic","labels":["phase:2","area:server","area:agent","kind:design","td"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"TD: client-server worker architecture — workers as long-lived API clients; server restart preserves in-flight work","updated_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z"}
{"acceptance":"1. New route POST /api/projects/{project}/graphql registered via scoped() middleware in cli/internal/server/server.go (parallel to existing /graphql trusted route).
2. The scoped route resolves the {project} path parameter via existing project context (workingDirForRequest or equivalent); rejects unregistered project with 404.
3. The route's GraphQL handler uses the request's project's WorkingDir (NOT s.WorkingDir) for all resolvers invoked through it. Implementation may either (a) reconstruct resolver per-request with the right WorkingDir, or (b) thread the WorkingDir through context — either is acceptable for LAYER 1.
4. Existing /graphql trusted route behavior unchanged (regression test).
5. Test: TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_DocumentByPath_OnlyReturnsScopedProjectDocs — register two projects A and B; query DocumentByPath via /api/projects/B/graphql; assert only B's documents are returned.
6. Test: TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_RejectsUnregisteredProject — query against /api/projects/unknown/graphql returns 404.
7. Test: TestGraphQL_LegacyRoute_StillWorks — assert /graphql still serves the startup project's data unchanged.
8. Manual verification: start server in project A, register project B, navigate to /nodes/local/projects/B in the frontend; verify documents/beads/executions shown via the scoped endpoint are B's, not A's. (May require a frontend one-line change to query the scoped endpoint when projectId is present; if so, include in this bead.)
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.

Followup beads filed for LAYER 2 and LAYER 3 (IDs noted in this bead's notes).","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:01:08Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","created_at":"2026-05-03T00:17:13.364353175Z","description":"LAYER 1 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. The full LAYER 1/2/3 plan was originally one bead; codex queue-hygiene review (2026-05-03) approved decomposition because LAYER 1 alone closes the HIGH-severity security gap and should ship without waiting for LAYERS 2-3.

ROOT CAUSE
The DDx server is single-project at initialization but multi-project-aware at runtime. HTTP REST endpoints properly enforce project boundaries via scoped()/legacy() middleware (cli/internal/server/server.go:712-719) and workingDirForRequest() (cli/internal/server/server.go:651-656). MCP tools resolve project correctly via mcpResolveWorkingDir() (cli/internal/server/server.go:590-612).

GraphQL bypasses ALL of this. The resolver is initialized once at cli/internal/server/server.go:4701 with WorkingDir: s.WorkingDir (server's startup project) and that field is immutable. The /graphql route is registered as `trusted("POST /graphql", ...)` not `scoped()`, so it cannot accept a {project} path parameter. The HIGH-severity exposure is at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_documents.go:26 — DocumentByPath always reads from r.WorkingDir.

LAYER 1 SCOPE
Add a parallel route /api/projects/{project}/graphql:
- Registered via scoped() middleware
- Resolves the {project} path param via existing project context infrastructure
- Reconstructs the GraphQL resolver with the request's project's WorkingDir per request (or threads it via context to the existing resolver)
- Existing /graphql endpoint behavior unchanged for backward compat

This is the immediate security fix. Frontend routes /nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/... can switch their GraphQL client to point at the scoped endpoint when projectId is present.

NOT IN LAYER 1 (deferred to LAYERS 2 + 3 — separate beads)
- LAYER 2: refactor resolver to take WorkingDir from request context across ALL resolvers (not just per-route reconstruction). Allows the existing /graphql endpoint to safely serve multiple projects.
- LAYER 3: implement Bead/Execution resolution in Node(id) Relay lookup with project-aware lookups (resolver_node.go:86-101 currently returns nil for bead IDs).

WHY DECOMPOSE
- LAYER 1 ships the security fix in one bead worth of work
- LAYER 2 is a larger refactor that doesn't need to block the security fix
- LAYER 3 is completeness; not security-critical
- All three could land independently if needed (LAYER 2/3 don't depend on each other)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-03T02:01:08.981081264Z","id":"ddx-4c51d33e","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:server","area:graphql","kind:fix","security","observed-failure"],"notes":"LAYER 2 follow-up: ddx-055e8d32 (resolver context refactor). LAYER 3 follow-up: ddx-5ae050dc (Node(id) Bead/Execution resolution). Decomposition per codex queue-hygiene review (2026-05-03).","owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":0,"status":"in_progress","title":"graphql LAYER 1: add /api/projects/{project}/graphql scoped route — closes HIGH-severity DocumentByPath cross-project leak","updated_at":"2026-05-03T02:01:08.981081264Z"}
{"acceptance":"1. Node(id) at cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go resolves Bead IDs (prefix "bead-") via the bead store scoped to context WorkingDir.
2. Node(id) resolves Execution run IDs (prefix "exec-" or whatever the run-id format is) via the executions store scoped to context WorkingDir.
3. Returns nil + appropriate error for IDs that don't match any known prefix.
4. Test: TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject — register two projects with overlapping bead IDs; query Node("bead-X") via project A's scoped GraphQL; assert returns project A's bead, not B's.
5. Test: TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject — same shape for execution runs.
6. Test: TestNode_ReturnsNilForUnknownPrefix — assert no panic, nil returned.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:08.83276105Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-5ae050dc","depends_on_id":"ddx-4c51d33e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:15Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-5ae050dc","depends_on_id":"ddx-055e8d32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:15Z"}],"description":"LAYER 3 of the GraphQL cross-project leak fix. Completeness work — fixes incomplete Relay spec compliance.

PROBLEM
cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go:86-101 (Node(id) Relay lookup) returns nil for any ID that isn't "node-" or "proj-" prefixed. Beads and Executions implement the Node interface in the schema but have no resolver. This breaks Relay clients that expect Node(id) lookup to work for any object type.

LAYER 3 SCOPE
Implement Node(id) resolution for:
- Bead IDs (e.g., "bead-ddx-abc12345")
- Execution run IDs (e.g., "exec-20260502T220000-abc1")

Resolution must respect the current project context (LAYER 2 dependency — without context-based WorkingDir, Node(id) can't scope correctly across projects).

NOT IN SCOPE
- Adding new node types beyond Bead/Execution
- Schema changes
- Backward-compat for clients that currently rely on nil-returning Node(id)

DEP: ddx-4c51d33e (LAYER 1) AND the LAYER 2 bead — Node(id) needs context-based WorkingDir to scope correctly.","id":"ddx-5ae050dc","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:server","area:graphql","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":3,"status":"open","title":"graphql LAYER 3: implement Bead/Execution Node(id) Relay resolution with project-aware lookups","updated_at":"2026-05-03T01:27:15.805880766Z"}

No changes needed: ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1 implementation is already present in this worktree's base (committed in e72f48c6 "chore: update tracker (execute-bead 20260503T020109)"). All AC are satisfied by existing code:

AC #1 — scoped POST/GET /api/projects/{project}/graphql routes registered at cli/internal/server/server.go:898-899 via the scoped() helper.
AC #2 — projectScoped middleware (cli/internal/server/server.go:619-629) resolves {project} and writes 404 for unknown.
AC #3 — handleGraphQLQueryScoped (cli/internal/server/server.go:4710-4713) calls serveGraphQL with workingDirForRequest(r), and serveGraphQL (4719-4758) constructs a per-request resolver whose WorkingDir + ExecLogs adapter use the resolved project's path. Implementation chose option (a): per-request resolver reconstruction.
AC #4 — legacy POST/GET /graphql trusted routes unchanged (server.go:892-893), still call serveGraphQL with s.WorkingDir.
AC #5/#6/#7 — TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_DocumentByPath_OnlyReturnsScopedProjectDocs, TestGraphQL_ScopedRoute_RejectsUnregisteredProject, TestGraphQL_LegacyRoute_StillWorks all live in cli/internal/server/graphql_scoped_route_test.go and pass.
AC #9 — `go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/server/graphql/...` green (verified locally this attempt).
